<|11.54|> And I realized in that moment the tragedy of it,<|16.76|>
<|17.42|> but also how vital it is, how it is we imagine with whom we are connected.<|22.90|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> How it is we imagine heaven.<|3.42|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> is a covenant and a linking with a very particular God who is revealed in a very particular story.<|8.04|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It's that God, not just some abstract God.<|4.26|>
<|6.06|> And it's the story of the God who liberates, the story of a God who gets passed down and loved, especially by anyone who,<|18.20|>

<|15.36|> The promised land, in part, in part, is the place of human flourishing, right?<|23.00|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We come here for many reasons, even complex reasons, but at our best we come here in order to remember the story, the story of who we are and whose we are.<|13.76|>
<|15.18|> This remembrance, however, is followed by a question, or it should be followed by a question, a moral question, a question.<|25.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Are we willing to share with others what we have received in this place?<|8.68|>
<|9.76|> The prayers, the relationships, the stories, the grace, the sacraments.<|19.72|>
<|19.72|> Are we willing to share all of it?<|21.60|>
<|21.70|> The place in the pews, room, room.<|24.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Are we willing to share all of it with another person, with another group?<|5.74|>
<|6.42|> It's perhaps true that you and I will only have received it, the grace, the gift, the covenant, when we long to share it.<|18.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And that is why the ultimate symbol in Christianity is not stone, but bread.<|6.54|>
